title: Issue-lifecycle state machine + transitions + cascade
description: Formal shape of workflow.yaml — the five issue-lifecycle states (todo, backlog, in-progress, review, done), the gated transitions between them (who authorises, who executes, what severity gates apply), the cascade rules (upward-only forward and closure cascade; no downward), and the closure triggers (PR merge into main, PR merge into integration branch, manual won't-do). Consumed by the transition-state skill to walk the machine, and by the validator to enforce authorisation gates. Companion per COR-018.

states.description: States in linear order from filing to closure. Order is load-bearing: forward cascade infers 'ancestor is behind' from this ordering.

transitions.description: Allowed transitions. A transition not listed here is forbidden.

cascade.description: The three cascade behaviours. Forward and closure cascade upward through ancestors; downward cascade is explicitly disallowed (parent state changes don't propagate to children).

closure_triggers.description: Recognised paths by which an issue reaches the Done state.

display_name.description: Display form used in board UIs and prose (e.g., `Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done`).

meaning.description: One-line definition of what an issue in this state represents.

applies_to.description: Issue-type tokens for which this state is reachable. Empty array would mean the state applies to no type and is therefore unused; populated lists name the types that pass through this state. Review is task-only; the other four states apply to every issue type.

inferred_from.description: Prose naming the native GitHub primitives the canonical state is derived from (issue open/closed, Milestone assignment, branch existence, PR state). Board fields are a projection — when native and board disagree, native wins.

to.description: Target state id (in-schema ref into `states`). Equal to `from` for PR sub-lifecycle rows (no issue-state change, only a `pr_state_effect`).

command.description: The verb-subject workflow-wrapper command that owns this transition (per DEC-026). Forward-progress wrappers: promote-issue / start-work / create-draft / review-work / back-to-draft / done-work. Won't-do closure rows use `close-issue` (outside the seven-command palette per DEC-026 rationale). Other commands (handoff-issue) don't appear here because they don't drive any transition.

pr_state_effect.description: The PR-state effect the owning command drives. Values: `none → none` (no PR effect), `none → draft` (create-draft), `none → ready | draft → ready` (review-work — opens new or flips draft→ready), `ready → draft` (back-to-draft), `ready → merged` (done-work). PR state is coupled to issue state; this field captures the coupling explicitly at the schema level.

authorisation.description: `user` = user holds the gate; agent may not flip without explicit authorisation (session-scoped). `agent-autonomous` = agent flips without prompting.

executor.description: Which actor performs the mutation. The user can both authorise and execute (rare); the agent typically executes either way.

applies_to.description: Issue-type tokens for which this transition is valid.

severity.description: Severity the agent applies when a user-gated transition is attempted without authorisation.

notes.description: Optional prose. Edge cases, examples, integration-branch variants.

transition.description: One allowed transition. Per DEC-026 schema_version 2, every transition declares its owning workflow-wrapper command and PR-state effect — including no-issue-transition rows for PR sub-lifecycle events (`from == to`).

direction.description: Which way the cascade propagates. `upward` = child change affects ancestor; `downward` = parent change affects child; `none` = disallowed.

behaviour.description: Prose describing the agent's action when a triggering condition is met.

applies_to_levels.description: Issue-type tokens the cascade walks through as ancestors / descendants. Empty array means the cascade is disabled.

idempotent.description: Whether re-firing the cascade on already-cascaded ancestors is a no-op. Forward cascade: true.

requires_user_authorisation.description: Whether the cascade prompts the user before mutating each level. Closure cascade: true (per-level).

cascade_rule.description: One of the three cascade behaviours.

target_state.description: Final state the trigger drives the issue to (always `done` at v0.1.0; field present for forward-compatibility).

applies_to.description: Issue-type tokens the trigger applies to.

mechanism.description: Prose describing the trigger's mechanics — what user action fires it, what the agent does in response, what auto-closes via GitHub.
